content.text: Claude API calls are inherently stateless: each
request is independent and the response is self-contained. This makes Claude service pods

— any pod can handle any request, with no sticky sessions, no shared disk, and no migration cost when pods are rescheduled. Kubernetes Deployments are the right abstraction: they manage replica count, rolling updates, and self-healing.

attrs.alt: K8s deployment: Ingress → ClusterIP Service → 3 claude-api pods (HPA 2-10) → Secret + ConfigMap + Anthropic API

content.text: based on actual profiling. Claude API calls are I/O-bound (waiting on HTTPS), so CPU requests are low. Memory usage is proportional to response size — for streaming responses with large max_tokens, size accordingly.

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: claude-api
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: claude-api
    version: "1.0.0"
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: claude-api
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1          # allow one extra pod during update
      maxUnavailable: 0    # never drop below replica count during update
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: claude-api
    spec:
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60   # finish in-flight requests
      containers:
        - name: claude-api
          image: your-registry/claude-api:1.0.0
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8000
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu:    "500m"
              memory: "256Mi"
            limits:
              cpu:    "1000m"
              memory: "512Mi"
          env:
            - name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: anthropic-credentials
                  key:  api-key
            - name: MODEL
              valueFrom:
                configMapKeyRef:
                  name: claude-config
                  key:  model
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8000
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds:       10
            failureThreshold:    3
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8000
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds:       20
            failureThreshold:    3
          lifecycle:
            preStop:
              exec:
                command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 5"]  # drain connections before SIGTERM

Set maxUnavailable: 0 and maxSurge: 1 for Claude services. A rolling update that takes down any replica before the new one is ready means users see errors during deploy — Claude API calls are expensive to retry.
Never bake the Anthropic API key into the container image or pass it as a plain env var in the Deployment spec. Use a Kubernetes
, which is base64-encoded at rest and can be rotated without redeploying.

# Secret (apply once; rotate by re-applying with new key)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: anthropic-credentials
  namespace: production
type: Opaque
stringData:
  api-key: "sk-ant-..."   # kubectl apply encrypts this

---

# ConfigMap for non-sensitive tunables
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: claude-config
  namespace: production
data:
  model:      "claude-opus-4-8"
  max-tokens: "4096"
  log-level:  "info"
  timeout-s:  "120"

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: claude-api
  namespace: production
spec:
  selector:
    app: claude-api
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 8000
  type: ClusterIP

---

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: claude-api
  namespace: production
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-read-timeout: "120"   # match Claude timeout
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-send-timeout: "120"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
    - hosts: ["api.example.com"]
      secretName: tls-example-com
  rules:
    - host: api.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: claude-api
                port:
                  number: 80
on the Ingress to match or exceed your Claude
parameter. The default nginx timeout of 60 seconds will cut off streaming responses for long completions.
Claude API pods are I/O-bound, not CPU-bound. Scale on
(via Prometheus + custom metrics adapter) or on average response latency. CPU-based HPA alone underscales because a pod waiting on Claude's HTTPS response uses almost no CPU.

# HPA using custom metric: requests per second per pod
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: claude-api-hpa
  namespace: production
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: claude-api
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
    - type: Pods
      pods:
        metric:
          name: http_requests_per_second
        target:
          type:         AverageValue
          averageValue: "20"   # scale out when avg > 20 RPS per pod
  behavior:
    scaleUp:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 60    # react quickly to traffic spikes
      policies:
        - type:          Pods
          value:         2
          periodSeconds: 60
    scaleDown:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 300   # avoid flapping on short dips
The readiness and liveness probes hit /healthz. Keep this endpoint lightweight — it must respond in milliseconds and must NOT make a Claude API call.

from fastapi import FastAPI
import anthropic, os

app = FastAPI()
client = anthropic.Anthropic()  # reuse across requests

@app.get("/healthz")
async def health():
    """Lightweight health check — no external calls."""
    return {
        "status": "ok",
        "model":  os.getenv("MODEL", "claude-opus-4-8"),
    }

@app.get("/readyz")
async def readiness():
    """
    Deeper readiness check — verifies the API key is present.
    Still does not make a Claude call (that would be too slow).
    """
    key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "")
    if not key.startswith("sk-ant-"):
        from fastapi import HTTPException
        raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail="API key not configured")
    return {"status": "ready"}
Claude API calls can take 30–120 seconds. Pods must drain in-flight requests before terminating. Set
longer than your p99 response time and use a
sleep to let the Service remove the pod from load balancing before SIGTERM fires.

import signal, asyncio
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

shutdown_event = asyncio.Event()

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # Startup
    yield
    # Shutdown — wait for in-flight requests to complete
    shutdown_event.set()

app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)

def handle_sigterm(sig, frame):
    # Signal uvicorn to stop accepting new connections
    # and wait for in-flight requests to finish
    asyncio.get_event_loop().call_soon_threadsafe(shutdown_event.set)

signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, handle_sigterm)
Measure actual memory usage with kubectl top pod during load testing before finalising limits. Claude streaming responses buffer tokens in memory; very large max_tokens values increase peak memory.
to the same path: a slow Claude call that delays health checks can cause cascading pod restarts. Use
: any user whose request hits the terminating pod gets an error. Set
: SIGKILL fires before Claude's response completes. Set it to at least
CPU-only HPA: Claude pods are I/O-bound. CPU rarely spikes. Use RPS-based custom metrics for accurate scaling.

Sharing the Anthropic API key across environments via one Secret: use separate Secrets per namespace (staging, production) to prevent a misconfigured staging deployment from exhausting the production key's rate limit.

Kubernetes Deployments are a natural fit for Claude services because they are stateless. Use RollingUpdate with maxUnavailable: 0 to avoid errors during deploys. Store the API key in a Secret, tunables in a ConfigMap. Scale horizontally using an HPA based on RPS rather than CPU — Claude pods spend most of their time waiting on HTTPS, not computing. Set terminationGracePeriodSeconds generously so in-flight Claude calls complete before the pod is killed.
